Start a build
Open for focused engineering work

I build systems that hold up under real load.

I work on trading infrastructure, fintech products, automation, and data integrity layers. The common requirement is the same every time: the software has to stay correct when timing, state, and human trust are all under pressure.

Start a project Read the case studies
Execution flows that reconcile cleanly across multiple runtimes and user surfaces.
Operator tooling that exposes system state clearly instead of hiding it behind polish.
Architectures built around one source of truth, predictable recovery paths, and readable audits.
6 years in production work Web, mobile, fintech, automation, and cross-system architecture.
20 plus shipped builds From landing surfaces to systems where state drift costs money and trust.
ss-dev / system-check.ts 
// The contract is clear before the build starts.
const constraints = [
  'single source of truth',
  'readable operator state',
  'recovery paths that make sense',
];

const result = 'software that behaves in the real world';
What I actually build

Proof first, then polish.

I am not selling a broad agency menu. The work is specific. It usually involves systems that sync across tools, teams, or platforms and break trust quickly if the data goes stale.

Trading and signal infrastructure

Execution paths, reconciliation logic, dashboards, and source-of-truth layers that stay aligned across runtimes.

Fintech and trust systems

Products where contributions, payouts, balances, and auditability need to stay boringly correct.

Operator tooling

Internal software that helps humans see the right state quickly instead of guessing through noise.

Selected work

Two systems that show how I think.

Both projects deal with trust. The first depends on technical correctness across distributed parts. The second depends on financial clarity for the people using it.

Trading infrastructure

SMC SuperFIB

A distributed system that keeps a Pine Script indicator, a PHP and WordPress API, MT5 expert advisors, and a React dashboard aligned around one source of truth.

React Node.js MT5 WordPress API
  • Reduced drift between trading views by designing backend authority into the system from the start.
  • Handled time normalization, staleness gates, and cross-platform parity so decisions stayed auditable.
  • Built operator visibility into the dashboard so state could be understood without reading raw logs.
Read the SuperFIB case study
Community finance

Stokvel Society

A fintech platform for member contributions, trust-layer ledger handling, and predictable payout flows where clarity matters as much as the interface.

Astro Fintech UX Ledger logic Reporting
  • Kept the core flow readable for members while protecting the underlying accounting logic.
  • Designed the platform around contribution visibility, trust, and predictable payout handling.
  • Turned a community finance workflow into software that can be monitored and explained clearly.
Read the Stokvel Society case study
Architecture stance

I design for failure paths before I design for screenshots.

The visuals matter, but the real work is elsewhere. I want the system to answer the hard questions cleanly when the data is late, a dependency fails, or a human needs to understand what happened.

One source of truth

When several tools touch the same state, I establish authority early so the system can reconcile instead of arguing with itself.

Operator readability

I expose the information that matters to the person running the system, not just the person who built it.

Recoverable behavior

Failures should produce understandable states and reasonable next actions, not mystery or panic.

How the work runs

A small process with very little theater.

I keep the path visible. You know what is being built, what it costs, and where the risk lives before the project starts drifting.

Discovery I clarify the problem first.

I ask what has to be correct, what can fail safely, and what constraints are non-negotiable.

System map I define the moving parts.

You get a plan, a boundary for the work, and a view of the technical choices before the build begins.

Build cadence I ship in readable increments.

Progress stays visible and the architecture stays coherent instead of being hidden in a last-minute dump.

Hardening I finish with checks that matter.

The end of the build is about stability, clarity, and operational confidence, not rushed cosmetics.

Work with me

If the system has to stay trustworthy, I am interested.

Tell me what you are building, what state needs to stay aligned, and where the risk sits. I will tell you quickly whether I am the right fit.

Email hello@ss-developers.co.za

I handle project discussions directly.
Location Cape Town, South Africa

Remote-first, with a preference for focused builds over broad retainer work.

SS Developers

I build trading, fintech, and data-heavy systems for teams that need the software to stay correct when the environment gets messy.
